name: Engram Chat

releases.author: PixelAnay
releases.description: Bugs fixed: Edit button reference issue, open memory root folder creation, and added regenerate button.
releases.name: v5.0.52

releases.author: PixelAnay
releases.description: This release resolves all 30 Medium Severity bugs in the Engram plugin.

releases.author: PixelAnay
releases.description:
## Fixes in this release

### Critical: Context history was being lost after long chats
Every AI turn was overwriting the full chat history with a truncated working set (limited to maxRecentMessages). Older messages were silently deleted from disk after each send. Opening a long chat next day would show only the last N messages. Fixed: only new messages from each turn are appended; complete history is always preserved.

### Token bar was underestimating context usage
The bar missed the system prompt tokens (persona + memory + vault map). It now shows system tokens plus recent chat tokens - exactly what the API receives each turn.

### Model struggled to edit or delete memories
Added a clear Reading memory IDs section to the system prompt explaining the ID format with an example. Improved delete_memory error message to be actionable when an invalid ID is given.
releases.name: v5.0.50 - Critical context reset fix, accurate token bar & memory UX

releases.author: PixelAnay
releases.description:
## What's New

### Chat history now lives in Intelligence/Chats/

Your vault's Intelligence/ folder now contains everything Engram manages:

`
Intelligence/
├── Memory.md        ← existing memory file
└── Chats/
    ├── chat-xxx.json
    ├── chat-yyy.json
    └── ...
`

The old .engram/chats/ hidden folder caused sync issues with iCloud, Windows file systems, and some Git setups because dot-folders are often excluded or invisible. The new Intelligence/Chats/ location is fully visible and syncs cleanly with every tool.

### Automatic migration

On first load after this update, any existing chats in .engram/chats/ are **automatically moved** to Intelligence/Chats/. The old empty .engram/ folder is then deleted. You'll see a brief notice confirming how many sessions were moved. No action required.

### Fully backward compatible

- Users on older versions still have their sessions migrated from data.json → Intelligence/Chats/
- The folder path is still configurable in **Settings → Advanced → Chat History**
releases.name: v5.0.49 - Chat history moved to Intelligence/Chats

releases.author: PixelAnay
releases.description:
## Bug Fixes

### .engram/chats is now the true single source of truth

- **Fix: Deleting a chat now permanently removes its .json file** — previously ault.delete() moved the file to the OS trash without removing it from the vault index, making it appear as if nothing was deleted. Now uses  orce: true for immediate, permanent removal.

- **Fix: Empty 'New chat' sessions are no longer written to disk** — only sessions that have at least one message get a file in .engram/chats/. This prevents ghost files from accumulating every time you open a new chat.

- **Fix: Removed stale timer field** from SessionManager left over from the pre-refactor code.

- **Improve: syncChatsFromVault reads purely from disk** — the folder is now the authoritative state; no in-memory-only sessions leak into the sync result.

- **Improve: Smarter handling of externally deleted sessions** — if a session is deleted on another device and synced, the plugin auto-switches to the next available chat instead of showing a blank screen.
releases.name: v5.0.48 - Chat folder sync fix

releases.author: PixelAnay
releases.description:
## What's New

### Chat History Now Syncs With Your Vault

Chat sessions are now stored as individual JSON files inside a hidden .engram/chats/ folder in your vault root — instead of the plugin's private data.json. This means your entire chat history syncs automatically with **any sync tool you already use**: iCloud, Obsidian Sync, Dropbox, Git, and more.

### Key Changes

- **Vault-backed persistence**: Each chat session is its own .engram/chats/<id>.json file. Only modified sessions cause sync conflicts.
- **Automatic migration**: Existing sessions in data.json are migrated to vault files on first startup — nothing is lost.
- **Live sync detection**: When a synced file arrives from another device, the session list updates instantly without disrupting your active chat.
- **Manual sync**: New command-palette entry **'Sync chat history from vault'** and a **'Sync now'** button in Advanced Settings.
- **Configurable path**: The folder path (default: .engram/chats) can be changed in **Settings → Advanced → Chat History**.

### Backward Compatibility

Fully backward compatible. Existing chat history is migrated automatically and silently on the first load after this update.
releases.name: v5.0.47 - Chat History Vault Sync

releases.author: PixelAnay
releases.description:
## What's Changed in 5.0.46

### ⌨️ Autocomplete Selection via Enter / Tab
- Fixed an issue where navigating to files using the @ mention dropdown and pressing Enter or Tab did nothing. Selecting highlighted files keyboard-wise now behaves identically to clicking them.

### 📄 Text and Markdown Attachments Support
- Enabled attaching external .txt, .md, .json, .js, .ts, and other text-based files using the attachment feature.
- Read files as plain text instead of base64 data URLs to prevent API crashes, and formatted their contents cleanly inline within the LLM prompt inside markdown code blocks.

### 📥 Drag & Drop File Attachments
- Added full Drag & Drop support to the sidebar chat panel. Users can drag and drop any supported files (images, PDFs, text, markdown files) directly onto the panel to attach them.
- Included a beautiful, animated, pulse-glow overlay that appears when dragging files over the chat panel.
releases.name: 5.0.46 - Autocomplete Selection Fix, Text Attachments & Drag & Drop

releases.author: PixelAnay
releases.description:
## What's Changed in 5.0.45 💾 Softened Memory Path Redirect for read_note
- Allowed the AI to perform 
read_note on the memory path if it specifically needs to verify same-turn modifications (e.g. immediately after a save_memory or delete_memory call).
- The tool output still includes an informational warning that memory is already loaded in the system context to deter unnecessary reads, but it now appends the live, current content of memory.md so lookup succeeds in the same turn.
🔄 Stated Turn-Refreshing of Memory
- Clarified in context.ts system prompt memory instructions that the memory block is reloaded fresh at the start of every message turn.
releases.name: 5.0.45 - Memory Read Softening & Turn Refresh Instruction

releases.author: PixelAnay
releases.description:
## What's Changed

### Memory Storage Guardrails (Stricter)
- The AI is now explicitly told that memory.md is the **ONE AND ONLY** location for storing personal facts. It must never save memories to any other note or folder.
- System prompt now contains a dedicated **Memory Rules** section with both storage and lookup rules clearly stated.
- ead_note called on the memory file path now returns a redirect message instead of raw content, preventing redundant reads.

### Memory-First Lookup (More Efficient)
- The AI is instructed to check the **already-loaded memory block first** before calling search_vault or ead_note for any personal/preference queries.
- search_vault tool description updated: explicitly states it should NOT be used for memory lookups.
- ead_note tool description updated: explicitly states it should NOT be used to read the memory file or look up personal facts.
- Memory block header now includes: *'This memory is already loaded — do NOT use search_vault or read_note to look up the memory file again.'*

These changes reduce unnecessary tool calls and prevent the AI from getting confused about where memories are stored.
releases.name: 5.0.44 - Stricter Memory Location Enforcement

releases.author: PixelAnay
releases.description:
## Fix: Input area stuck disabled after errors

Fixed a bug where the chat input textarea could become permanently non-editable. This happened when an error occurred during context building (before the streaming phase) — the `setStreaming(false)` call was never reached, leaving the input stuck in a disabled state.

### Changes
- Wrapped all code after `setStreaming(true)` in `sendMessage()` inside a `try/finally` block so the input is always re-enabled, even on errors
- Reset `isStreaming` state in `onClose()` to prevent stale state when Obsidian reuses the view instance
- Reset `isStreaming` in `onOpen()` as a safety net for view reopening

releases.author: PixelAnay
releases.description:
## Engram v5.0.0

> **Breaking change**: Plugin renamed from LLAMA Chat to Engram. Please reinstall via BRAT if upgrading.

### What's new

**Multi-provider AI support**
- Works with any AI: local (llama.cpp, Ollama, LM Studio) or cloud (OpenAI, Claude, DeepSeek, Mistral, Groq, xAI, Gemini, OpenRouter)
- 2 adapters cover 100% of providers (OpenAI-compatible + dedicated Anthropic adapter)
- Switch providers from Settings with a dropdown — no config files

**Persistent memory system**
- Remembers facts about you across all sessions
- Stored in your vault as \Intelligence/Memory.md\ — visible and editable
- Auto-extracts memorable facts silently after each conversation turn
- Use \/memory\ to manually trigger extraction at any time
- Structured sections: Goals, Beliefs, Habits, Projects, Learnings, Identity

**Persona system**
- 4 built-in personas: Default, Deep Work, Journaling, Brainstorm
- Create, edit, and delete custom personas from Settings
- Switch instantly with \/persona\ or the 🎭 button in the header

**Slash commands**
- \/memory\ — Save facts from current conversation to memory
- \/forget\ — Open memory file to edit/delete entries
- \/persona\ — Switch active persona
- \/export\ — Export conversation to a vault note
- \/clear\ — Clear current session
- \/scope\ — Show current vault access scope

**Folder scope permissions**
- Allow-list mode: restrict AI to specific folders only
- Deny-list mode: block specific folders (previous behaviour preserved)
- Applied at indexing, tool-call, and context levels

**Cost-optimised context**
- Memory-first: only memory + recent messages sent by default
- Notes loaded on-demand via tools (not pre-loaded into every message)
- Auto-inject set to 0 by default — safe for cloud APIs

**Settings redesign** — 6 sections: AI Provider, Persona, Memory, Vault Access, Context & Performance, Semantic Search

---
*Install via [BRAT](https://github.com/TfTHacker/obsidian42-brat): add \PixelAnay/localLLM_Obsidian\*
releases.name: 5.0.0 - Engram: The AI That Remembers You

releases.author: PixelAnay
releases.description:
Full architectural overhaul of the LLAMA Chat plugin.

**What's new in v4.0.0:**
- Critical bug fixes: replace_block occurrence selection, undo for delete/move/rename, path traversal protection
- Security: vault content sandboxed against prompt injection, real user confirmation for destructive ops
- Performance: debounced streaming render, cached vault map, incremental DOM updates, chunked full-text search
- Architecture: ChatView refactored from 1200 to ~430 lines, 8 new focused modules extracted
- UX: token budget bar, dynamic welcome chips, selective undo history panel, context-build status label
- TypeScript strict mode enabled (0 errors)
